title: Parkio Daily for July 13, 2026
teaser: Polynesian's new bus stop opens today; Frontierland boardwalk closes soon; Fort Wilderness pool area debuts.

breaking.title: Polynesian Village bus stop opens today
breaking.body: The redesigned bus stop at Disney's Polynesian Village Resort began operating this morning after months of construction. Guests now use the new facility for all park and resort transportation.

parkioInsight.text: Morning pickup times may be slightly longer today as guests adjust to the new layout. Arriving five minutes earlier than usual tends to smooth out any confusion during the first week.

bignews.title: Fort Wilderness unveils new pool area and refreshed lobby
bignews.body: Disney's Fort Wilderness Resort has completed a major update, introducing a redesigned pool complex and modernized lobby spaces. The resort's woodland theming remains intact throughout the refresh.

parkioInsight.text: The new pool area is likely to draw resort guests mid-afternoon. Visiting before 11am or after 5pm usually offers quieter access and shorter waits for poolside seating.

bignews.title: Magic Kingdom preparing to close Frontierland boardwalk shortcut
bignews.body: The Frontierland boardwalk walkway has been removed from park maps and will soon close entirely as construction work near the Rivers of America continues. The shortcut has been gradually restricted over recent months.

parkioInsight.text: Plan extra walking time between Liberty Square and Splash Mountain areas once the closure takes effect. Routing through the main Frontierland path adds roughly five minutes during moderate crowd levels.

bignews.title: Tomorrowland entrance pillars removed ahead of potential overhaul
bignews.body: Magic Kingdom quietly dismantled the final 1990s-era decorative pillars at Tomorrowland's entrance, marking another step in rumored land-wide renovations. The structures had stood since the land's last major redesign.

parkioInsight.text: Tomorrowland entrance areas may see minor foot-traffic rerouting during the work. Arriving via the carousel side rather than the main hub path often avoids bottlenecks when construction zones are active.

bignews.title: Hollywood Studios expands Muppets area with second project
bignews.body: Disney has launched additional development work in the Muppets courtyard at Hollywood Studios, following the recent debut of Rock 'n' Roller Coaster Starring The Muppets earlier this year. Details on the expansion remain unannounced.

parkioInsight.text: The current Muppets area is likely to see increased guest interest as word spreads. Visiting between rope drop and 10am usually offers the shortest waits for Rock 'n' Roller Coaster before midday crowds arrive.

topstories.title: Port Royal shop reopens with Halloween merchandise at Disneyland
topstories.body: Port Royal Curios and Curiosities has returned to New Orleans Square at Disneyland Park, now stocked with early Halloween items including character accessories and seasonal apparel. The space previously operated as a jazz club seating area.

parkioInsight.text: Halloween merchandise at Disneyland typically sells out quickly in flagship locations. Stopping by before noon today likely secures the best selection before popular items disappear.

topstories.title: Caribbean Beach debuts Little Mermaid-themed rooms
topstories.body: Disney's Caribbean Beach Resort has completed a reimagining of select guest rooms with Under the Sea theming inspired by The Little Mermaid. The update replaces the resort's previous pirate-themed accommodations.

parkioInsight.text: Newly themed rooms often book up weeks in advance once word spreads. Checking availability for fall and winter stays today may offer better selection before demand increases.

topstories.title: Tokyo Disney Resort announces fall Halloween event dates
topstories.body: Tokyo Disney Resort confirmed its annual Disney Halloween celebration will run from September 16 through November, featuring the returning Disney Villains parade, character Halloween Greeting, and the Coco-themed event.

parkioInsight.text: International park Halloween events often inspire merchandise and programming at domestic resorts. Watching for similar announcements at Walt Disney World and Disneyland over the next few weeks may help plan fall visits.

topstories.title: Sunshine Seasons adds two new menu items at EPCOT
topstories.body: The counter-service restaurant Sunshine Seasons in EPCOT's The Land pavilion has introduced two fresh dishes to its rotating menu. The location continues to offer breakfast, lunch, and dinner options.

parkioInsight.text: Sunshine Seasons usually sees lighter lunch crowds than World Showcase restaurants. Arriving between 11:30am and noon often means shorter lines and immediate seating during the festival season.

topstories.title: Popular Disney World restaurant raises menu prices
topstories.body: An unnamed but frequently visited Walt Disney World restaurant updated its menu pricing this week, reflecting broader adjustments across resort dining locations. Specific increases were not detailed.

parkioInsight.text: Menu price changes tend to ripple across multiple locations within a few weeks. Locking in dining reservations or mobile orders today may secure current pricing before additional updates roll out.

topstories.title: Moana presence expands across Disney Parks globally
topstories.body: Disney Parks Blog highlighted the growing footprint of Moana-themed experiences worldwide, including live entertainment, character meet-and-greets, and immersive environments at domestic and international resorts.

parkioInsight.text: Character meet locations for Moana tend to see shorter waits during the first and last hours of park operation. Checking the app for current availability often reveals walk-up windows mid-morning.

topstories.title: Loungefly releases Inside Out 2 Ennui accessories online
topstories.body: Loungefly added a mini backpack and wallet featuring the character Ennui from Inside Out 2 to its online shop. Both items showcase the character's signature periwinkle color scheme and bubble motif.

parkioInsight.text: Limited-edition Loungefly drops often sell out within days of release. Ordering online today likely ensures availability before stock moves to in-park locations where selection may be thinner.

icymi.title: Disney celebrates America's 250th anniversary through July 4 weekend
icymi.body: The Walt Disney Company launched a nationwide initiative called Disney Celebrates America, running from Veterans Day 2025 through the July 4, 2026 weekend. The campaign features patriotic programming and in-park festivities.

icymi.title: Command Performance band returns to UK Pavilion at EPCOT
icymi.body: The Command Performance band resumed live performances at the United Kingdom Pavilion in EPCOT's World Showcase after a hiatus. The group plays British classics daily.

icymi.title: Tinker Bell pixie dust collection launches at Loungefly
icymi.body: Loungefly released a new Tinker Bell-themed collection featuring black velvet bags with gold appliques of stars, pixie dust, and crescent moons. The line is available online.

spotlight.title: Fourth of July weekend wait times defied expectations
spotlight.body: Wait times at Walt Disney World over the Independence Day holiday weekend tracked lower than anticipated despite high attendance, suggesting operational adjustments and guest distribution patterns shifted from prior years.

parkioInsight.text: Summer weekdays immediately following major holidays often see lighter crowds as families return home. Visiting Monday through Wednesday this week may offer shorter waits across all four parks before the next weekend surge.
